articles.title: Weather Relief for Telangana and Andhra Pradesh - The Hans India
articles.full_text:
Weather Relief for Telangana and Andhra Pradesh
The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has announced a welcome change in weather for residents of Telangana and Andhra Pradesh, who have been enduring intense heat over recent days. A surface trough has triggered the likelihood of thunderstorms with lightning over the next two to three days, bringing relief from the scorching temperatures.
For the past week, temperatures in Telangana soared to record highs, leaving many residents feeling stifled. However, the weather has now cooled due to the formation of a trough ahead of the Southwest Monsoon’s arrival. The IMD reports that light to moderate rains are expected across several districts today and tomorrow, which should result in a temperature drop of approximately 2 to 3 degrees Celsius.
The Meteorological Centre has indicated that the skies over Greater Hyderabad and surrounding districts will remain overcast. The heat is forecast to ease in the afternoon, giving way to more pleasant conditions. During rainfall, gusty winds of 30 to 40 km/h may occur. Some parts of the city have already experienced light showers, prompting the GHMC administration to prepare for possible waterlogging in low-lying areas.
Heavy rains are also expected across Andhra Pradesh. The IMD warns that districts such as Visakhapatnam, Polavaram, Anakapalli, Kakinada, East Godavari, Konaseema, and Vizianagaram could experience thunderstorms accompanied by lightning. Meanwhile, a trough extending across Rayalaseema and Tamil Nadu is likely to bring cooler weather to those districts.
